[Home](../)

---
title: "HW 7"
format:
  html:
    output-file: "index"
embed-resources: true
---

**Exercise 1**

a) Guidlines for creating Bar charts
- Bars must start at zero baseline
- For long labels, use horizontal bars instead of rotating labels
- Order bars by ascending or descending value when categories have no natural ordering

b) It is not advised to use a Bar chart when the data values are all large and similar. This is because it is hard to see the differences.

c) For large and similar datasets, for alternatives we can use heatmaps or dot plot.

d) Wilke advises to use stacked bars when sum of the values or meaningful (counts, total). When comparing seperate values dodged bars or separate bars are more appropriate. 

```{r}
library(vegawidget)

movies <- list(
  `$schema` = "https://vega.github.io/schema/vega-lite/v6.json",
  width = 500,
  data = list(url = "https://calvin-data304.netlify.app/data/cow-movies.csv"),
  mark = "bar",
  encoding = list(
    x = list(field = "amount", type = "quantitative", 
             title = "weekend gross (million USD)",
             axis = list(format = "~s")),
    y = list(field = "title_short", type = "nominal",
             sort = list(field = "amount", order = "descending"),
             title = NULL)
  )
)

as_vegaspec(movies)
```
